coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Build Phase, 16 weeks (112 days) to Julian Alps. Friday is your pre-weekend primer: 50–60 min easy Zone 2 (HR ~135–145) with 4–5 × 20s strides on a slight incline to wake the legs without taxing them. Keep it conversational — tomorrow's long run and Sunday's back-to-back are where the alpine adaptation happens. Traveling? A treadmill at 4–6% covers the strides.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Start prepping the gut for the weekend's big efforts. Target ~6–7 g/kg carbs today (~450–550g): oats + banana at breakfast, a rice or pasta bowl at lunch, sweet potato + protein at dinner. Lock in a tested race breakfast you can repeat Saturday morning, and rehearse fueling on the long run — 60–90g carbs/hr. Train the gut as seriously as the legs.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Protect tonight's sleep — you want 8+ hours before back-to-back long days. If you've crossed time zones this week, grab 10–15 min of morning daylight to anchor your rhythm. After today's run, foam-roll calves, quads, and glutes for 8–10 min so the legs are springy for this weekend's descents. Hydrate to pale-yellow by 6pm.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Build Phase is where the race is quietly won — consistency now compounds into confidence on the Kranjska Gora start line. The ~2,500m of vert and variable alpine weather reward athletes who logged the boring middle blocks. This weekend's back-to-back simulates running on tired legs; treat fatigue as a teacher, not a threat. Show up, do the work, trust the bank you're building.
